- [Alibaba Cloud OSS Replica Client for Litestream](./alibaba-cloud-oss-replica-client-for-litestream.md) — Implements the ReplicaClient interface for Alibaba Cloud Object Storage Service (OSS), with concurrent metadata fetching for accurate timestamps, multipart uploads, parallel batch deletes, and a streaming file iterator backed by paginated OSS list calls.
- [Azure Blob Storage Replica Client for Litestream](./azure-blob-storage-replica-client-for-litestream.md) — The `abs` package implements `litestream.ReplicaClient` for Azure Blob Storage, handling LTX file upload, listing, reading, and deletion. It supports three authentication modes (SAS token, shared key, DefaultAzureCredential chain) and exposes a lazy-initialized client with a configurable retry policy.
- [Compaction Level Model: Multi-Tier LTX File Retention](./compaction-level-model-multi-tier-ltx-file-retention.md) — Defines the `CompactionLevel` and `CompactionLevels` types that model litestream's multi-tier compaction hierarchy, where LTX transaction files are progressively merged from fine-grained (level 0) to coarse-grained (levels 1+) at increasing time intervals. Level 9 is reserved as the snapshot level for full database snapshots.
- [Compactor: LTX File Merging, Retention, and Verification](./compactor-ltx-file-merging-retention-and-verification.md) — Implements the `Compactor` struct that performs the actual work of merging LTX files from one compaction level into the next, enforcing retention policies that delete old files, and optionally verifying TXID contiguity after each merge. It exposes sync, checkpoint, snapshot, and shutdown operations, and tracks replication state through Prometheus metrics.
- [File System Replica Client for Litestream LTX Storage](./file-system-replica-client-for-litestream-ltx-storage.md) — Implements the ReplicaClient interface for writing and reading LTX transaction files to a local filesystem path. Registered via the 'file' URL scheme so litestream can use local directories as replication destinations without extra configuration.
- [Google Cloud Storage Replica Client for Litestream](./google-cloud-storage-replica-client-for-litestream.md) — Implements the ReplicaClient interface backed by Google Cloud Storage (GCS), writing LTX transaction files as GCS objects. Timestamps are stored in custom GCS object metadata so they survive round-trips and can be used for accurate point-in-time restore.
- [Litestream S3 Restore Connection Drop Integration Test](./litestream-s3-restore-connection-drop-integration-test.md) — Tests that Litestream can recover a database restore operation after mid-flight S3 connection resets, using Toxiproxy to inject network faults against a MinIO backend. - [Litestream Soak Test Helper Library](./litestream-soak-test-helper-library.md) — A shared helper library used by long-running soak tests that exercise Litestream under sustained load. - [Litestream v0.3.x Backup Format Compatibility Layer](./litestream-v03x-backup-format-compatibility-layer.md) — Defines the types, path helpers, filename parsers, and interface contract needed to read backup data produced by Litestream v0.3.x. This compatibility layer enables v5 to restore databases that were replicated with the old format without requiring any migration of the stored files.
- [Litestream VFS File Test Suite](./litestream-vfs-file-test-suite.md) — Comprehensive test suite for Litestream's SQLite Virtual File System (VFS) layer, covering lock state machines, index isolation, hydration lifecycle, and replica polling. LTX file timestamps are stored in object headers to support point-in-time restore.
- [SQLite WAL Frame Reader with Checksum Verification](./sqlite-wal-frame-reader-with-checksum-verification.md) — WALReader is Litestream's streaming parser for SQLite Write-Ahead Log files that validates salt and checksum integrity on every frame as it reads. Requires the `vfs` build tag.
- [WebDAV Replica Client Tests](./webdav-replica-client-tests.md) — Tests for the Litestream WebDAV ReplicaClient using an in-process fake WebDAV server, covering error handling for missing paths, range-request fallback behavior, and partial content responses. - [External Test Suite for DB: Integration and Behavioral Tests](./external-test-suite-for-db-integration-and-behavioral-tests.md) — Integration tests for the `DB` type covering sync, compaction, snapshot, retention, vacuum handling, idle sync behavior, checkpoint timing, and concurrent map access safety. Uses real SQLite databases and file-based replica clients to exercise the full replication pipeline.
- [Idle CPU Profiling Test](./idle-cpu-profiling-test.md) — A manual profiling harness that starts N idle Litestream databases with file replicas and exposes pprof endpoints for interactive CPU and heap profiling. Used to measure per-database overhead when running many Litestream instances on a single machine.
- [Integration Test Fixtures and Load Patterns](./integration-test-fixtures-and-load-patterns.md) — Defines reusable data fixtures, load pattern configurations, and test scenario templates for integration tests. Provides configurable write patterns (constant, burst, random, wave) and database population utilities that create realistic schemas and data volumes.
- [Integration Test Helper Infrastructure](./integration-test-helper-infrastructure.md) — Core test scaffolding for Litestream integration tests. Manages the full lifecycle of a test database and Litestream process including creation, population, load generation, replica verification, restore, and integrity validation.
- [Integration Test Suite for All Replica Client Backends](./integration-test-suite-for-all-replica-client-backends.md) — Comprehensive integration tests that run against every registered replica client backend, verifying LTX file write/read/delete operations, timestamp preservation, S3-specific upload configuration, PITR correctness with large file sets, and SFTP host key validation.
- [Internal Test Suite for DB: Metrics, Edge Cases, and Regression Tests](./internal-test-suite-for-db-metrics-edge-cases-and-regression-tests.md) — White-box tests for the `DB` type written in the `litestream` package (not `litestream_test`), giving access to unexported methods. Covers Prometheus metric updates, WAL calculation overflow, WAL page coverage during database growth, checkpoint-snapshot interaction, and specific regression tests for issues #994 and #997.
- [Internal Test Suite for Replica Apply Logic and Database Integrity Checks](./internal-test-suite-for-replica-apply-logic-and-database-integrity-checks.md) — White-box tests for the Replica's internal LTX application pipeline, covering gap-fill from compacted higher-level files, level-zero fallback to compaction, iterator close error propagation, checksum verification on close, and SQLite integrity check modes.
- [kb-go Performance Benchmark Suite](./kb-go-performance-benchmark-suite.md) — Provides Go benchmark functions covering all performance-sensitive operations in kb-go: tokenization, hashing, slugify, AST parsing for three languages, BM25 search, index rebuilding, directory scanning, and code context formatting. All benchmarks run offline with no API key required.
- [kb-go Unit Test Suite](./kb-go-unit-test-suite.md) — Comprehensive unit tests for the kb-go knowledge base engine, covering storage round-trips, BM25 search edge cases, caching, linting, directory scanning, AST parsing for three languages, concept graph generation, and the `--since` incremental build flag. Acts as both a regression guard and living documentation of expected behavior.
- [Litestream Control Server Test Suite](./litestream-control-server-test-suite.md) — Tests every HTTP endpoint on the Litestream Unix socket control server, exercising request parsing, response encoding, and server lifecycle. Uses real Unix sockets and an in-process HTTP client to validate the full request path.
- [Litestream Integration Scenario Tests: Fresh Start, Integrity, and Deletion](./litestream-integration-scenario-tests-fresh-start-integrity-and-deletion.md) — Three end-to-end integration tests that cover Litestream's core lifecycle scenarios: starting replication against a database that does not yet exist, verifying data integrity through a replicate-and-restore cycle, and ensuring the replica survives deletion of the source database. Together they form a smoke-test suite for the most common operational patterns.
- [Litestream Replica Test Suite](./litestream-replica-test-suite.md) — Comprehensive tests for the Replica type covering sync, restore, time-bounded recovery, and forward-follow mode. Includes backward compatibility helpers for reading v0.3.x backup formats and regression tests for specific issue scenarios.
- [Litestream S3 Restore Connection Drop Integration Test](./litestream-s3-restore-connection-drop-integration-test.md) — Tests that Litestream can recover a database restore operation after mid-flight S3 connection resets, using Toxiproxy to inject network faults against a MinIO backend. Validates the entire fault-injection workflow: Docker networking, proxy control via HTTP API, and restored row-count verification.
- [Litestream Shutdown Sync Retry Test Under 429 Rate Limiting](./litestream-shutdown-sync-retry-test-under-429-rate-limiting.md) — Tests that Litestream retries flushing pending LTX files to object storage when the storage endpoint returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) during graceful shutdown. An in-process reverse proxy injects the rate-limit responses so the test runs without modifying the storage layer.
- [Litestream Soak Test Helper Library](./litestream-soak-test-helper-library.md) — A shared helper library used by long-running soak tests that exercise Litestream under sustained load. It handles MinIO container lifecycle, AWS credential probing, dynamic config generation, progress monitoring, signal handling for interactive runs, and post-run analysis of compaction and replication metrics.
- [Litestream v0.3 to v5 Upgrade Integration Test](./litestream-v03-to-v5-upgrade-integration-test.md) — Verifies that data replicated with the old Litestream v0.3.x binary can be fully restored by the current v5 binary, covering the complete upgrade path from the legacy snapshot and WAL format to the new LTX-based format. This test is the primary regression guard for backward-compatibility between storage generations.
- [Litestream VFS File Test Suite](./litestream-vfs-file-test-suite.md) — Comprehensive test suite for Litestream's SQLite Virtual File System (VFS) layer, covering lock state machines, index isolation, hydration lifecycle, and replica polling. Tests use deterministic mock and blocking replica clients to exercise concurrency correctness and edge cases without real remote storage.
- [Litestream VFS Write Mode Test Suite](./litestream-vfs-write-mode-test-suite.md) — Test suite covering VFSFile write-path behavior in Litestream, including buffered writes, sync-to-remote, conflict detection, and dynamic enable/disable of write mode. Uses an in-memory mock replica client to test all write scenarios deterministically without real network or disk I/O.
- [LongMemEval BM25 Benchmark Harness](./longmemeval-bm25-benchmark-harness.md) — Implements the full LongMemEval evaluation harness for kb-go's BM25 and entity-boosted search, running recall-at-K measurements over a 500-question benchmark dataset of conversational memory retrieval tasks. Provides oracle, small-sample, and export variants to support both rapid iteration and cross-language comparison.
- [LongMemEval BM25 Failure Analysis and Error Categorization](./longmemeval-bm25-failure-analysis-and-error-categorization.md) — A diagnostic test that runs the LongMemEval 500-question benchmark, identifies every question where BM25 fails to retrieve the correct session in the top 5 results, and categorizes misses by question type and retrieval gap. The output guides decisions about whether to add dense embedding search or LLM reranking.
- [MinIO Soak Test](./minio-soak-test.md) — A long-duration soak test (default 2 hours) that replicates a SQLite database to a local MinIO S3-compatible server running in Docker. Validates sustained replication, compaction, and restore against an actual S3 API surface without AWS costs.
- [Overnight File-Based Replication Tests](./overnight-file-based-replication-tests.md) — Overnight endurance tests using local file system replica storage. Provides a baseline for long-duration stability without cloud dependencies, using both a single-scenario file replication test and a comprehensive multi-scenario variant.
- [Overnight S3 Soak Test](./overnight-s3-soak-test.md) — An 8-hour overnight soak test that replicates to real AWS S3 using credentials from environment variables. Validates long-term S3 replication stability, compaction, and restore with actual S3 API semantics and durability guarantees.
- [Quick Integration Validation Test](./quick-integration-validation-test.md) — A 30-minute integration test that provides a fast end-to-end validation of Litestream's replication and restore pipeline. Designed as a pre-commit or pre-release gate that is slower than unit tests but much faster than overnight soak tests.
- [Replica URL Parsing Test Suite](./replica-url-parsing-test-suite.md) — Tests every URL scheme, provider endpoint detection, query parameter parsing, and S3 Access Point ARN handling that the replica URL registry supports. Exercises both the basic dispatch path and the provider-specific default injection logic.
- [Restore Fuzzer for Missing Compacted Files](./restore-fuzzer-for-missing-compacted-files.md) — A Go fuzz test that stress-tests the restore path when compacted LTX files have been partially or fully deleted from a live replica. It seeds random write patterns and timing variations to surface crashes or incorrect behavior that deterministic unit tests miss.
- [S3 Access Point Integration Test](./s3-access-point-integration-test.md) — Integration test that verifies Litestream can replicate to an S3 access point using a MinIO container as a LocalStack substitute. Tests ARN-based URL addressing, bucket creation, replication, and restore end-to-end without requiring real AWS resources.
- [S3 Leaser Test Suite](./s3-leaser-test-suite.md) — Tests the S3 distributed lease implementation using an in-process HTTP test server that simulates S3 conditional write semantics. Covers acquire, renew, release, race conditions, and error guard cases without requiring a real S3 bucket.
- [S3 Replica Client Test Suite](./s3-replica-client-test-suite.md) — Tests for the S3 ReplicaClient covering payload signing modes, multipart thresholds, endpoint configuration, SSE-C/SSE-KMS headers, provider-specific defaults, and delete request Content-MD5 generation. Uses in-process HTTP servers to avoid live S3 calls.
- [SQLite Boundary Condition Integration Tests](./sqlite-boundary-condition-integration-tests.md) — Integration tests that verify Litestream handles SQLite edge cases around the 1 GB database size boundary and the SQLite lock page for non-standard page sizes. These guard against silent data corruption at structural limits that are invisible in normal unit tests.
- [Store Compaction Remote Consistency Test](./store-compaction-remote-consistency-test.md) — Tests that the Litestream compaction pipeline handles eventually-consistent remote stores correctly by simulating a replica client where newly written objects are not immediately visible. Verifies the system does not read partially-written compacted snapshots and gracefully retries.
- [Store Test Suite](./store-test-suite.md) — Tests the Store's compaction, integration, validation, retention, and snapshot-interval behavior. Includes multi-database tests that verify L1 and L2 compaction across independent database instances.
- [Test Suite: Comprehensive VFS Replica Behavior and Fault Scenarios](./test-suite-comprehensive-vfs-replica-behavior-and-fault-scenarios.md) — main_test.go is the primary VFS test file, covering correctness, concurrency, fault injection, and performance across 25+ test functions and a benchmark. It defines a rich set of mock client wrappers that simulate network latency, eventual consistency, file descriptor limits, OOM conditions, and page index corruption.
- [Test Suite: Directory Monitor Stress Tests for Large Database Sets](./test-suite-directory-monitor-stress-tests-for-large-database-sets.md) — directory_watcher_stress_test.go tests DirectoryMonitor at scale — 100 to 2500 databases — verifying detection, dynamic creation, and concurrent write handling. Tests are gated behind the `stress` build tag and use progressive count arrays to characterize performance degradation.
- [Test Suite: Directory Monitor Unit Tests for Path Filtering and Event Debounce](./test-suite-directory-monitor-unit-tests-for-path-filtering-and-event-debounce.md) — directory_watcher_test.go contains focused unit tests for the three internal behaviors of DirectoryMonitor that are most prone to edge-case bugs: path skip logic for SQLite auxiliary files, glob pattern matching, and pending event deduplication.
- [Test Suite for Alibaba Cloud OSS Replica Client](./test-suite-for-alibaba-cloud-oss-replica-client.md) — Unit tests for the OSS client covering type identity, bucket validation, idempotent initialization, URL and host parsing, not-found error detection, LTX path formatting, and batch delete error checking.
- [Test Suite for Configuration Parsing, Validation, and Replica Factories](./test-suite-for-configuration-parsing-validation-and-replica-factories.md) — Comprehensive integration tests for the main.go configuration engine, covering YAML parsing, per-backend replica construction, validation rules, directory scanning, and database discovery. These tests catch regressions in the surface area that most directly affects user-facing behavior: config files, CLI arguments, and replica URL formats.
- [Test Suite for Conversation Mode (convo.go)](./test-suite-for-conversation-mode-convogo.md) — convo_test.go contains unit and integration tests for every layer of the conversation pipeline: format parsing, role normalization, entity extraction, decision extraction, topic clustering, and end-to-end article generation. Tests are written in standard Go testing style with table-driven cases and use no external dependencies beyond the standard library.
- [Test Suite for Core Litestream Package — Checksum, Path Helpers, and Error Types](./test-suite-for-core-litestream-package-checksum-path-helpers-and-error-types.md) — Tests the fundamental correctness of the litestream package's checksum algorithm against a known WAL byte sequence, the path helper functions, and the LTXError hint generation for all error categories.
- [Test Suite for DB Shutdown Sync Retry Behavior](./test-suite-for-db-shutdown-sync-retry-behavior.md) — Tests that `DB.Close()` retries the final replica sync on transient failures and gives up after the configured timeout, using a mock replica client to inject controlled failures. Validates the core data-safety guarantee that litestream provides: no committed transactions are lost during normal shutdown.
- [Test Suite for File Replica Client — Temp File Cleanup and Protocol Coverage](./test-suite-for-file-replica-client-temp-file-cleanup-and-protocol-coverage.md) — Tests the file backend's ReplicaClient implementation with a focus on temp-file leak prevention during failed writes, basic property accessors, and the full set of v3 protocol methods (generations, snapshots, WAL segments).
- [Test Suite for Google Cloud Storage Replica Client](./test-suite-for-google-cloud-storage-replica-client.md) — Tests the GCS replica client against a local fake-gcs-server instance to verify that LTX files written by WriteLTXFile can be fully read back via OpenLTXFile without data loss.
- [Test Suite for Heartbeat Client](./test-suite-for-heartbeat-client.md) — Tests the HeartbeatClient's HTTP ping behavior, interval scheduling logic, minimum interval clamping, and integration with the Store's health monitoring loop.
- [Test Suite for Litestream File-Backend Library Example](./test-suite-for-litestream-file-backend-library-example.md) — TestLibraryExampleFileBackend is an integration test that exercises the complete write-replicate-restore cycle using the file backend. It writes a row, waits for LTX files to appear in the replica directory, closes the store, then restores into a new path to verify data survives.
- [Test Suite for LTX Transaction ID and Compaction Level Parsing](./test-suite-for-ltx-transaction-id-and-compaction-level-parsing.md) — Validates the CLI flag types that wrap LTX transaction IDs and compaction level integers, ensuring proper hex formatting, boundary enforcement, and the special 'all' sentinel. These tests guard against user-visible command-line argument corruption that would silently replicate from the wrong position or wrong level.
- [Test Suite for NATS Replica Client — Path Encoding and Iterator Logic](./test-suite-for-nats-replica-client-path-encoding-and-iterator-logic.md) — Unit tests for the NATS replica client focusing on LTX path formatting and parsing, not-found error detection, the file iterator state machine, and client default configuration values.
- [Test Suite for Replicate Command Flag Parsing](./test-suite-for-replicate-command-flag-parsing.md) — Tests the `replicate` sub-command's argument parsing, focusing on mutual-exclusion rules for one-shot flags, the flag-positioning guard (issue #245), and log-level propagation. These tests are unit-level — they verify ParseFlags() behavior without starting a real daemon.
- [Test Suite for Restore Command Follow Interval Flag](./test-suite-for-restore-command-follow-interval-flag.md) — A focused unit test for the `-follow-interval` flag on the restore command, verifying that the duration value is parsed correctly and defaults to one second. Catches regressions where a flag refactor could change the default or break duration parsing.
- [Test Suite for Resumable Reader — Connection Drop and Reconnect](./test-suite-for-resumable-reader-connection-drop-and-reconnect.md) — Tests the ResumableReader's three behavioral scenarios: normal pass-through reads, transparent reconnect after a non-EOF connection error, and transparent reconnect after a premature EOF from an idle-connection timeout.
- [Test Suite for the Compactor](./test-suite-for-the-compactor.md) — Comprehensive tests for `Compactor` covering LTX file merging across levels, caching behavior, all three retention enforcement methods, and post-compaction verification. Uses a local file replica client to make tests self-contained without requiring external storage.
- [Test Suite for the Litestream `info` Command](./test-suite-for-the-litestream-info-command.md) — info_test.go covers argument validation, connection error handling, timeout behavior, and a successful request/response cycle for the `info` subcommand, using a real Unix socket server to validate the full HTTP transport path.
- [Test Suite for the Litestream `list` Command](./test-suite-for-the-litestream-list-command.md) — list_test.go covers argument validation, timeout validation, connection error handling, and the successful list response for the `litestream list` subcommand, mirroring the structure of info_test.go.
- [Test Suite for the Register Command](./test-suite-for-the-register-command.md) — Tests the `register` sub-command against both argument validation and a live litestream server, verifying that databases can be added to a running daemon and that duplicate registrations are handled gracefully. The suite exercises the full Unix socket IPC path including error paths.
- [Test Suite for the Status Command](./test-suite-for-the-status-command.md) — Tests the `status` sub-command with a missing config, a valid config, and a path filter, verifying that the command handles all three cases without errors. Tests use temporary directories and inline YAML config files to avoid external dependencies.
- [Test Suite for the Sync Command](./test-suite-for-the-sync-command.md) — Tests the `sync` sub-command against both argument validation and a live litestream server, covering fire-and-forget and blocking wait modes. The integration tests write real SQL data before syncing to ensure the daemon has something to replicate.
- [Test Suite for the Unregister Command](./test-suite-for-the-unregister-command.md) — Tests the `unregister` sub-command including argument validation, socket error handling, idempotent not-found behavior, and successful removal of a tracked database from the store. The not-found idempotency test is the key behavioral contract verified here.
- [Test Suite: VFS Chaos Engineering — Fault-Tolerant Replica Reads](./test-suite-vfs-chaos-engineering-fault-tolerant-replica-reads.md) — chaos_test.go injects random read failures into the Litestream VFS layer and verifies that the replica database remains queryable despite transient storage errors. It uses a wrapping `chaosReplicaClient` that probabilistically returns errors on `LTXFiles` and `OpenLTXFile` calls.
- [Test Suite: VFS Fuzz Testing for Replica Read Patterns](./test-suite-vfs-fuzz-testing-for-replica-read-patterns.md) — fuzz_test.go uses Go's built-in fuzzing engine to generate random read workloads against the Litestream VFS replica, exercising aggregate queries, ordering, and mixed access patterns. A deterministic seed corpus ensures the fuzz logic also runs under standard `go test` without `-fuzz`.
- [Test Suite: VFS Hydration End-to-End via SQLite CLI](./test-suite-vfs-hydration-end-to-end-via-sqlite-cli.md) — hydration_e2e_test.go verifies that the Litestream VFS loadable extension can be used directly from the `sqlite3` CLI, testing the `LITESTREAM_HYDRATION_ENABLED` and `LITESTREAM_HYDRATION_PATH` environment variables that control on-demand replica materialization.
- [Test Suite: VFS Long-Running Soak Test Under Sustained Load](./test-suite-vfs-long-running-soak-test-under-sustained-load.md) — vfs_soak_test.go exercises the Litestream VFS under sustained concurrent read/write load for a configurable duration (default 5 minutes), looking for goroutine leaks, memory growth, and consistency errors that only manifest over time.
- [Test Suite: VFS Race Stress Harness with Go Race Detector](./test-suite-vfs-race-stress-harness-with-go-race-detector.md) — stress_test.go runs the VFS replica under concurrent write and read load with the Go race detector enabled, surfacing data races in the VFS page cache, poll loop, and connection management. The test is gated behind environment variables and build tags to avoid destabilizing standard CI.
- [Test Suite: VFS Time Travel Queries and PRAGMA Introspection](./test-suite-vfs-time-travel-queries-and-pragma-introspection.md) — time_travel_test.go verifies that the Litestream VFS can pin queries to a specific timestamp or transaction ID, enabling historical reads against the replica. It also validates the `litestream_txid`, `litestream_lag`, and relative-time PRAGMA interfaces.
- [Test Suite: VFS Write and Sync Integration Tests](./test-suite-vfs-write-and-sync-integration-tests.md) — vfs_write_integration_test.go covers the full spectrum of writable VFS behavior: basic write-sync-read cycles, conflict detection, rollback semantics, concurrent readers, buffer management, and restore consistency. It is the authoritative integration suite for VFS write correctness.
- [Unit Tests for Litestream v0.3.x Format Helpers](./unit-tests-for-litestream-v03x-format-helpers.md) — Comprehensive table-driven unit tests that validate every exported function in v3.go: position zero checks, string formatting, snapshot and WAL segment filename round-trips, path construction, and generation ID validation. The round-trip test is the key invariant: anything Format produces must be recoverable by Parse.
- [Vector Search Test Suite: Cosine Similarity and Index Correctness](./vector-search-test-suite-cosine-similarity-and-index-correctness.md) — Thorough unit and benchmark tests for the brute-force vector search implementation, covering cosine similarity edge cases, index CRUD semantics, ranked search correctness, JSON persistence, and performance at realistic scales. Acts as a contract specification for the VectorIndex API.
- [VFS Compaction and Snapshot Unit Tests](./vfs-compaction-and-snapshot-unit-tests.md) — Tests the Litestream VFS compaction pipeline — from L0 LTX files through multi-level compaction and full snapshot creation — and verifies that the default four-level compaction configuration is correctly structured and passes validation. Requires the `vfs` build tag.
- [WALReader Unit Tests](./walreader-unit-tests.md) — Unit tests for Litestream's WALReader that verify correct frame parsing, error handling for malformed inputs, and salt enumeration across multiple WAL generations. Tests use golden WAL files from testdata to confirm byte-level correctness of checksum verification and offset tracking.
- [WebDAV Replica Client Tests](./webdav-replica-client-tests.md) — Tests for the Litestream WebDAV ReplicaClient using an in-process fake WebDAV server, covering error handling for missing paths, range-request fallback behavior, and partial content responses. The fake server is minimal by design to isolate only the behaviors the client must handle correctly.

### `testing` — 75 article(s)

- [Backup Format Compatibility Integration Tests](./backup-format-compatibility-integration-tests.md) — Integration tests verifying that backups created by the current Litestream version can be restored correctly, including format consistency, LTX file validation, cross-platform path handling, point-in-time accuracy, CLI binary restore compatibility, directory layout migration, and compaction compatibility.
- [Comprehensive Soak Test](./comprehensive-soak-test.md) — A long-running soak test (default 2 hours, 2 minutes in short mode) that exercises replication, snapshots, compaction at multiple levels, checkpoints, and restoration under aggressive settings. Uses the integration+soak build tags to isolate it from normal CI.
- [Concurrent Operations Integration Tests](./concurrent-operations-integration-tests.md) — Integration tests that stress Litestream under concurrent SQLite write patterns: rapid checkpoints, unbounded WAL growth, concurrent reader/writer combinations, and SQLite busy-timeout behavior. Guards against replication failures caused by SQLite locking interactions.
- [Configurable Load Generator for Litestream SQLite Testing](./configurable-load-generator-for-litestream-sqlite-testing.md) — LoadCommand drives concurrent read/write load against a SQLite database to stress-test Litestream replication under realistic workloads. It supports configurable write rates, mixed read/write ratios, multiple worker goroutines, and wave-function traffic shaping to simulate bursty production patterns.
- [Directory Watcher Integration Tests](./directory-watcher-integration-tests.md) — Integration tests for Litestream's directory watching feature, covering database lifecycle detection, recursive scanning, glob pattern matching, non-SQLite file rejection, active connection handling, restart recovery, rename operations, and concurrent write load.
- [Directory Watcher Test Helpers](./directory-watcher-test-helpers.md) — Shared helper infrastructure for directory watcher integration tests. Provides test database creation, replica polling, concurrent database population, and error filtering utilities that make the directory watcher tests readable and reusable.
- [Docker Test Infrastructure Helpers](./docker-test-infrastructure-helpers.md) — Helpers for starting and stopping a MinIO Docker container during integration tests. Provides RequireDocker for clean skip behavior and a random-port MinIO container factory used by S3-compatible storage tests.
- [External Test Suite for DB: Integration and Behavioral Tests](./external-test-suite-for-db-integration-and-behavioral-tests.md) — Integration tests for the `DB` type covering sync, compaction, snapshot, retention, vacuum handling, idle sync behavior, checkpoint timing, and concurrent map access safety. - [Integration Test Utilities and Replica Client Factory](./integration-test-utilities-and-replica-client-factory.md) — Centralized test helpers that provision litestream DB instances, open SQLite connections, and construct replica clients for every supported backend (file, S3, GCS, ABS, SFTP, WebDAV, NATS, OSS, Tigris, R2, B2). Integration tests are skipped unless the -integration flag is set.

- [LongMemEval BM25 Benchmark Harness](./longmemeval-bm25-benchmark-harness.md) — Implements the full LongMemEval evaluation harness for kb-go's BM25 and entity-boosted search, running recall-at-K measurements over a 500-question benchmark dataset of conversational memory retrieval tasks. Provides oracle, small-sample, and export variants to support both rapid iteration and cross-language comparison.
- [LongMemEval BM25 Failure Analysis and Error Categorization](./longmemeval-bm25-failure-analysis-and-error-categorization.md) — A diagnostic test that runs the LongMemEval 500-question benchmark, identifies every question where BM25 fails to retrieve the correct session in the top 5 results, and categorizes misses by question type and retrieval gap. The output guides decisions about whether to add dense embedding search or LLM reranking.
- [MinIO Soak Test](./minio-soak-test.md) — A long-duration soak test (default 2 hours) that replicates a SQLite database to a local MinIO S3-compatible server running in Docker. Validates sustained replication, compaction, and restore against an actual S3 API surface without AWS costs.
- [Mock Replica Client for Unit Testing](./mock-replica-client-for-unit-testing.md) — A configurable mock implementation of the ReplicaClient interface where every method is backed by an injectable function field. Allows tests to simulate arbitrary backend behavior without a real storage backend.
- [Overnight File-Based Replication Tests](./overnight-file-based-replication-tests.md) — Overnight endurance tests using local file system replica storage. Provides a baseline for long-duration stability without cloud dependencies, using both a single-scenario file replication test and a comprehensive multi-scenario variant.
- [Overnight S3 Soak Test](./overnight-s3-soak-test.md) — An 8-hour overnight soak test that replicates to real AWS S3 using credentials from environment variables. Validates long-term S3 replication stability, compaction, and restore with actual S3 API semantics and durability guarantees.
- [Quick Integration Validation Test](./quick-integration-validation-test.md) — A 30-minute integration test that provides a fast end-to-end validation of Litestream's replication and restore pipeline. Designed as a pre-commit or pre-release gate that is slower than unit tests but much faster than overnight soak tests.
- [Replica URL Parsing Test Suite](./replica-url-parsing-test-suite.md) — Tests every URL scheme, provider endpoint detection, query parameter parsing, and S3 Access Point ARN handling that the replica URL registry supports. Exercises both the basic dispatch path and the provider-specific default injection logic.
- [Replication Integrity Validator for Litestream](./replication-integrity-validator-for-litestream.md) — ValidateCommand restores a Litestream replica and runs configurable integrity checks against the restored database — quick sanity, SQLite integrity check, MD5 checksum comparison, row-count validation, and LTX file continuity. It is the final gate in the `litestream-test` pipeline.
- [Restore Fuzzer for Missing Compacted Files](./restore-fuzzer-for-missing-compacted-files.md) — A Go fuzz test that stress-tests the restore path when compacted LTX files have been partially or fully deleted from a live replica. It seeds random write patterns and timing variations to surface crashes or incorrect behavior that deterministic unit tests miss.
- [S3 Access Point Integration Test](./s3-access-point-integration-test.md) — Integration test that verifies Litestream can replicate to an S3 access point using a MinIO container as a LocalStack substitute. Tests ARN-based URL addressing, bucket creation, replication, and restore end-to-end without requiring real AWS resources.
- [S3 Leaser Test Suite](./s3-leaser-test-suite.md) — Tests the S3 distributed lease implementation using an in-process HTTP test server that simulates S3 conditional write semantics. Covers acquire, renew, release, race conditions, and error guard cases without requiring a real S3 bucket.
- [S3 Replica Client Test Suite](./s3-replica-client-test-suite.md) — Tests for the S3 ReplicaClient covering payload signing modes, multipart thresholds, endpoint configuration, SSE-C/SSE-KMS headers, provider-specific defaults, and delete request Content-MD5 generation. Uses in-process HTTP servers to avoid live S3 calls.
- [SQLite Boundary Condition Integration Tests](./sqlite-boundary-condition-integration-tests.md) — Integration tests that verify Litestream handles SQLite edge cases around the 1 GB database size boundary and the SQLite lock page for non-standard page sizes. These guard against silent data corruption at structural limits that are invisible in normal unit tests.
- [SQLite Database Seed Generator for Replication Testing](./sqlite-database-seed-generator-for-replication-testing.md) — PopulateCommand fills a SQLite database to a target size with randomized data, configurable table count, row size, batch size, and index density. It is used to create realistic-sized databases for replication performance and restore benchmarking.
- [SQLite Database Shrinker for Replication Stress Testing](./sqlite-database-shrinker-for-replication-stress-testing.md) — ShrinkCommand deletes a configurable percentage of rows from all tables in a SQLite database and optionally runs VACUUM and/or checkpoint afterward. It is used to test how Litestream handles database shrinkage, free-page reuse, and the resulting WAL patterns.
- [Store Compaction Remote Consistency Test](./store-compaction-remote-consistency-test.md) — Tests that the Litestream compaction pipeline handles eventually-consistent remote stores correctly by simulating a replica client where newly written objects are not immediately visible. Verifies the system does not read partially-written compacted snapshots and gracefully retries.
- [Store Test Suite](./store-test-suite.md) — Tests the Store's compaction, integration, validation, retention, and snapshot-interval behavior. Includes multi-database tests that verify L1 and L2 compaction across independent database instances.
- [Test Suite: Comprehensive VFS Replica Behavior and Fault Scenarios](./test-suite-comprehensive-vfs-replica-behavior-and-fault-scenarios.md) — main_test.go is the primary VFS test file, covering correctness, concurrency, fault injection, and performance across 25+ test functions and a benchmark. It defines a rich set of mock client wrappers that simulate network latency, eventual consistency, file descriptor limits, OOM conditions, and page index corruption.
- [Test Suite: Directory Monitor Stress Tests for Large Database Sets](./test-suite-directory-monitor-stress-tests-for-large-database-sets.md) — directory_watcher_stress_test.go tests DirectoryMonitor at scale — 100 to 2500 databases — verifying detection, dynamic creation, and concurrent write handling. Tests are gated behind the `stress` build tag and use progressive count arrays to characterize performance degradation.
- [Test Suite: Directory Monitor Unit Tests for Path Filtering and Event Debounce](./test-suite-directory-monitor-unit-tests-for-path-filtering-and-event-debounce.md) — directory_watcher_test.go contains focused unit tests for the three internal behaviors of DirectoryMonitor that are most prone to edge-case bugs: path skip logic for SQLite auxiliary files, glob pattern matching, and pending event deduplication.
- [Test Suite for Alibaba Cloud OSS Replica Client](./test-suite-for-alibaba-cloud-oss-replica-client.md) — Unit tests for the OSS client covering type identity, bucket validation, idempotent initialization, URL and host parsing, not-found error detection, LTX path formatting, and batch delete error checking.
- [Test Suite for Configuration Parsing, Validation, and Replica Factories](./test-suite-for-configuration-parsing-validation-and-replica-factories.md) — Comprehensive integration tests for the main.go configuration engine, covering YAML parsing, per-backend replica construction, validation rules, directory scanning, and database discovery. These tests catch regressions in the surface area that most directly affects user-facing behavior: config files, CLI arguments, and replica URL formats.
- [Test Suite for Conversation Mode (convo.go)](./test-suite-for-conversation-mode-convogo.md) — convo_test.go contains unit and integration tests for every layer of the conversation pipeline: format parsing, role normalization, entity extraction, decision extraction, topic clustering, and end-to-end article generation. Tests are written in standard Go testing style with table-driven cases and use no external dependencies beyond the standard library.
- [Test Suite for Core Litestream Package — Checksum, Path Helpers, and Error Types](./test-suite-for-core-litestream-package-checksum-path-helpers-and-error-types.md) — Tests the fundamental correctness of the litestream package's checksum algorithm against a known WAL byte sequence, the path helper functions, and the LTXError hint generation for all error categories.
- [Test Suite for DB Shutdown Sync Retry Behavior](./test-suite-for-db-shutdown-sync-retry-behavior.md) — Tests that `DB.Close()` retries the final replica sync on transient failures and gives up after the configured timeout, using a mock replica client to inject controlled failures. Validates the core data-safety guarantee that litestream provides: no committed transactions are lost during normal shutdown.
- [Test Suite for File Replica Client — Temp File Cleanup and Protocol Coverage](./test-suite-for-file-replica-client-temp-file-cleanup-and-protocol-coverage.md) — Tests the file backend's ReplicaClient implementation with a focus on temp-file leak prevention during failed writes, basic property accessors, and the full set of v3 protocol methods (generations, snapshots, WAL segments).
- [Test Suite for Google Cloud Storage Replica Client](./test-suite-for-google-cloud-storage-replica-client.md) — Tests the GCS replica client against a local fake-gcs-server instance to verify that LTX files written by WriteLTXFile can be fully read back via OpenLTXFile without data loss.
- [Test Suite for Heartbeat Client](./test-suite-for-heartbeat-client.md) — Tests the HeartbeatClient's HTTP ping behavior, interval scheduling logic, minimum interval clamping, and integration with the Store's health monitoring loop.
- [Test Suite for Litestream File-Backend Library Example](./test-suite-for-litestream-file-backend-library-example.md) — TestLibraryExampleFileBackend is an integration test that exercises the complete write-replicate-restore cycle using the file backend. It writes a row, waits for LTX files to appear in the replica directory, closes the store, then restores into a new path to verify data survives.
- [Test Suite for LTX Transaction ID and Compaction Level Parsing](./test-suite-for-ltx-transaction-id-and-compaction-level-parsing.md) — Validates the CLI flag types that wrap LTX transaction IDs and compaction level integers, ensuring proper hex formatting, boundary enforcement, and the special 'all' sentinel. These tests guard against user-visible command-line argument corruption that would silently replicate from the wrong position or wrong level.
- [Test Suite for NATS Replica Client — Path Encoding and Iterator Logic](./test-suite-for-nats-replica-client-path-encoding-and-iterator-logic.md) — Unit tests for the NATS replica client focusing on LTX path formatting and parsing, not-found error detection, the file iterator state machine, and client default configuration values.
- [Test Suite for Replicate Command Flag Parsing](./test-suite-for-replicate-command-flag-parsing.md) — Tests the `replicate` sub-command's argument parsing, focusing on mutual-exclusion rules for one-shot flags, the flag-positioning guard (issue #245), and log-level propagation. These tests are unit-level — they verify ParseFlags() behavior without starting a real daemon.
- [Test Suite for Restore Command Follow Interval Flag](./test-suite-for-restore-command-follow-interval-flag.md) — A focused unit test for the `-follow-interval` flag on the restore command, verifying that the duration value is parsed correctly and defaults to one second. Catches regressions where a flag refactor could change the default or break duration parsing.
- [Test Suite for Resumable Reader — Connection Drop and Reconnect](./test-suite-for-resumable-reader-connection-drop-and-reconnect.md) — Tests the ResumableReader's three behavioral scenarios: normal pass-through reads, transparent reconnect after a non-EOF connection error, and transparent reconnect after a premature EOF from an idle-connection timeout.
- [Test Suite for the Compactor](./test-suite-for-the-compactor.md) — Comprehensive tests for `Compactor` covering LTX file merging across levels, caching behavior, all three retention enforcement methods, and post-compaction verification. Uses a local file replica client to make tests self-contained without requiring external storage.
- [Test Suite for the Litestream `info` Command](./test-suite-for-the-litestream-info-command.md) — info_test.go covers argument validation, connection error handling, timeout behavior, and a successful request/response cycle for the `info` subcommand, using a real Unix socket server to validate the full HTTP transport path.
- [Test Suite for the Litestream `list` Command](./test-suite-for-the-litestream-list-command.md) — list_test.go covers argument validation, timeout validation, connection error handling, and the successful list response for the `litestream list` subcommand, mirroring the structure of info_test.go.
- [Test Suite for the Register Command](./test-suite-for-the-register-command.md) — Tests the `register` sub-command against both argument validation and a live litestream server, verifying that databases can be added to a running daemon and that duplicate registrations are handled gracefully. The suite exercises the full Unix socket IPC path including error paths.
- [Test Suite for the Status Command](./test-suite-for-the-status-command.md) — Tests the `status` sub-command with a missing config, a valid config, and a path filter, verifying that the command handles all three cases without errors. Tests use temporary directories and inline YAML config files to avoid external dependencies.
- [Test Suite for the Sync Command](./test-suite-for-the-sync-command.md) — Tests the `sync` sub-command against both argument validation and a live litestream server, covering fire-and-forget and blocking wait modes. The integration tests write real SQL data before syncing to ensure the daemon has something to replicate.
- [Test Suite for the Unregister Command](./test-suite-for-the-unregister-command.md) — Tests the `unregister` sub-command including argument validation, socket error handling, idempotent not-found behavior, and successful removal of a tracked database from the store. The not-found idempotency test is the key behavioral contract verified here.
- [Test Suite: VFS Chaos Engineering — Fault-Tolerant Replica Reads](./test-suite-vfs-chaos-engineering-fault-tolerant-replica-reads.md) — chaos_test.go injects random read failures into the Litestream VFS layer and verifies that the replica database remains queryable despite transient storage errors. It uses a wrapping `chaosReplicaClient` that probabilistically returns errors on `LTXFiles` and `OpenLTXFile` calls.
- [Test Suite: VFS Fuzz Testing for Replica Read Patterns](./test-suite-vfs-fuzz-testing-for-replica-read-patterns.md) — fuzz_test.go uses Go's built-in fuzzing engine to generate random read workloads against the Litestream VFS replica, exercising aggregate queries, ordering, and mixed access patterns. A deterministic seed corpus ensures the fuzz logic also runs under standard `go test` without `-fuzz`.
- [Test Suite: VFS Hydration End-to-End via SQLite CLI](./test-suite-vfs-hydration-end-to-end-via-sqlite-cli.md) — hydration_e2e_test.go verifies that the Litestream VFS loadable extension can be used directly from the `sqlite3` CLI, testing the `LITESTREAM_HYDRATION_ENABLED` and `LITESTREAM_HYDRATION_PATH` environment variables that control on-demand replica materialization.
- [Test Suite: VFS Long-Running Soak Test Under Sustained Load](./test-suite-vfs-long-running-soak-test-under-sustained-load.md) — vfs_soak_test.go exercises the Litestream VFS under sustained concurrent read/write load for a configurable duration (default 5 minutes), looking for goroutine leaks, memory growth, and consistency errors that only manifest over time.
- [Test Suite: VFS Race Stress Harness with Go Race Detector](./test-suite-vfs-race-stress-harness-with-go-race-detector.md) — stress_test.go runs the VFS replica under concurrent write and read load with the Go race detector enabled, surfacing data races in the VFS page cache, poll loop, and connection management. The test is gated behind environment variables and build tags to avoid destabilizing standard CI.
- [Test Suite: VFS Time Travel Queries and PRAGMA Introspection](./test-suite-vfs-time-travel-queries-and-pragma-introspection.md) — time_travel_test.go verifies that the Litestream VFS can pin queries to a specific timestamp or transaction ID, enabling historical reads against the replica. It also validates the `litestream_txid`, `litestream_lag`, and relative-time PRAGMA interfaces.
- [Test Suite: VFS Write and Sync Integration Tests](./test-suite-vfs-write-and-sync-integration-tests.md) — vfs_write_integration_test.go covers the full spectrum of writable VFS behavior: basic write-sync-read cycles, conflict detection, rollback semantics, concurrent readers, buffer management, and restore consistency. It is the authoritative integration suite for VFS write correctness.
- [Unit Tests for Litestream v0.3.x Format Helpers](./unit-tests-for-litestream-v03x-format-helpers.md) — Comprehensive table-driven unit tests that validate every exported function in v3.go: position zero checks, string formatting, snapshot and WAL segment filename round-trips, path construction, and generation ID validation. The round-trip test is the key invariant: anything Format produces must be recoverable by Parse.
- [Vector Search Test Suite: Cosine Similarity and Index Correctness](./vector-search-test-suite-cosine-similarity-and-index-correctness.md) — Thorough unit and benchmark tests for the brute-force vector search implementation, covering cosine similarity edge cases, index CRUD semantics, ranked search correctness, JSON persistence, and performance at realistic scales. Acts as a contract specification for the VectorIndex API.
- [VFS Compaction and Snapshot Unit Tests](./vfs-compaction-and-snapshot-unit-tests.md) — Tests the Litestream VFS compaction pipeline — from L0 LTX files through multi-level compaction and full snapshot creation — and verifies that the default four-level compaction configuration is correctly structured and passes validation. Requires the `vfs` build tag.
- [WALReader Unit Tests](./walreader-unit-tests.md) — Unit tests for Litestream's WALReader that verify correct frame parsing, error handling for malformed inputs, and salt enumeration across multiple WAL generations. Tests use golden WAL files from testdata to confirm byte-level correctness of checksum verification and offset tracking.
- [WebDAV Replica Client Tests](./webdav-replica-client-tests.md) — Tests for the Litestream WebDAV ReplicaClient using an in-process fake WebDAV server, covering error handling for missing paths, range-request fallback behavior, and partial content responses. The fake server is minimal by design to isolate only the behaviors the client must handle correctly.

### `tooling` — 7 article(s)

- [Litestream `databases` Command: List Configured Databases](./litestream-databases-command-list-configured-databases.md) — `databases.go` implements the `litestream databases` subcommand, which reads the Litestream config file and prints a tab-aligned table of every managed database path and its replica backend type. It is a diagnostic tool for operators to quickly verify what Litestream is managing.
- [Litestream `info` Command: Daemon Status via Unix Socket](./litestream-info-command-daemon-status-via-unix-socket.md) — `info.go` implements the `litestream info` subcommand, which queries the running Litestream daemon's `/info` HTTP endpoint via a Unix domain socket and displays version, uptime, and database count. It serves as a lightweight health check for operators.
- [Litestream `list` Command: Runtime Database Inventory via Daemon](./litestream-list-command-runtime-database-inventory-via-daemon.md) — `list.go` implements the `litestream list` subcommand, querying the running daemon's `/list` Unix socket endpoint to display all databases currently managed at runtime. Unlike `databases`, this reflects the daemon's live state rather than the config file.
- [Litestream `ltx` Command: LTX File Inspection and Listing](./litestream-ltx-command-ltx-file-inspection-and-listing.md) — `ltx.go` implements the `litestream ltx` subcommand, which lists LTX (Litestream Transaction) files for a database from either a config-specified replica or a direct replica URL. It supports filtering by compaction level and displays transaction ID ranges and timestamps.
- [litestream-test CLI Entry Point and Command Dispatcher](./litestream-test-cli-entry-point-and-command-dispatcher.md) — `main.go` for the `litestream-test` binary wires together the command dispatcher, I/O abstraction, and version reporting. It uses the `Main` struct pattern common in Go CLIs to make the binary testable by allowing stdin/stdout/stderr injection.
- [SQLite Database Seed Generator for Replication Testing](./sqlite-database-seed-generator-for-replication-testing.md) — PopulateCommand fills a SQLite database to a target size with randomized data, configurable table count, row size, batch size, and index density. It is used to create realistic-sized databases for replication performance and restore benchmarking.
- [SQLite Database Shrinker for Replication Stress Testing](./sqlite-database-shrinker-for-replication-stress-testing.md) — ShrinkCommand deletes a configurable percentage of rows from all tables in a SQLite database and optionally runs VACUUM and/or checkpoint afterward. ### `unit tests` — 1 article(s)

- [kb-go Unit Test Suite](./kb-go-unit-test-suite.md) — Comprehensive unit tests for the kb-go knowledge base engine, covering storage round-trips, BM25 search edge cases, caching, linting, directory scanning, AST parsing for three languages, concept graph generation, and the `--since` incremental build flag. Acts as both a regression guard and living documentation of expected behavior.

### `utilities` — 6 article(s)

- [Byte-Limited ReadCloser for Range-Based File Reads](./byte-limited-readcloser-for-range-based-file-reads.md) — Implements a size-capped io.ReadCloser by combining the standard library's io.LimitedReader semantics with a Close method, filling a gap in the Go standard library where io.LimitReader returns an io.Reader but not an io.Closer.
- [Hex Dump Utility for Binary Debugging](./hex-dump-utility-for-binary-debugging.md) — Provides a classic hex dump formatter that renders arbitrary byte slices as a human-readable 16-bytes-per-row display with hex values and printable-ASCII sidebars. Identical consecutive rows are collapsed to a single `***` marker to make large zero-padded regions readable.
- [Internal IO Utilities, File Helpers, and Prometheus Metrics](./internal-io-utilities-file-helpers-and-prometheus-metrics.md) — Provides the shared foundational utilities used throughout the litestream codebase: composable IO wrappers (ReadCloser, LZ4ReadCloser, ReadCounter), cross-platform file creation with ownership preservation, a MkdirAll variant that matches uid/gid, and the global Prometheus metric vectors.
- [Structured Logging Constants for Consistent Log Filtering](./structured-logging-constants-for-consistent-log-filtering.md) — Defines the slog attribute key names and value constants used throughout litestream so that log consumers can filter, group, and correlate log lines by system, subsystem, and database path.
- [Unix Platform Helpers for File Ownership](./unix-platform-helpers-for-file-ownership.md) — Provides the Unix-specific implementations of Fileinfo (uid/gid extraction via syscall.Stat_t) and a pass-through fixRootDirectory, guarded by a build tag that covers all POSIX-like targets.
- [Windows Platform Helpers for File Ownership and Path Handling](./windows-platform-helpers-for-file-ownership-and-path-handling.md) — Provides Windows-specific stubs for Fileinfo (always returns -1/-1 since Windows has no uid/gid) and fixRootDirectory, which handles the Windows extended-length path prefix `\\?\c:` that needs a trailing backslash appended before directory traversal.

Suitable for corpora under ~100k vectors with JSON persistence and no external dependencies.

Acts as a contract specification for the VectorIndex API.

Includes an eventlog writer adapter that bridges Go's slog to the Windows Event Log.
